extraction.one_line_summary: MARTIN Dan attacked late on Laguna Negra category 1 climb, took 4 bonus seconds on ROGLIČ Primož to close within 5 seconds overall.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ riding unusually far back in peloton during Laguna Negra approach, uncharacteristic positioning suggests team confidence or fatigue management.
Movistar controlled valley section anticipating crosswinds after category 3, positioning for echelon aggression became standard tactic this race.
KUSS Sepp paced ROGLIČ on final climb, domestique strength critical when leader positioned defensively rather than at front.

rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: First win since twins born, carried Tour de France injury fitness through to exploit home terrain knowledge in Vuelta
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: Lost 4 bonus seconds despite red jersey retention, uncharacteristically deep positioning on climb approach reveals strategic caution
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Moved into solo KOM lead, positioning for stage podiums while contesting overall suggests dual ambitions remain viable
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Moved to fourth overall after teammate CHAVES lost time, Movistar leadership clarifying toward single protected rider
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: positive: Fifth overall through three brutal stages indicates genuine GC form beyond typical one-week racing level
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: Paced ROGLIČ on Laguna Negra finale, super-domestique role solidified as critical to defending narrow overall lead
rider_signals.CHAVES Esteban: negative: Lost time and dropped to outside top-5, Movistar protection shifting to MAS as co-leadership experiment fails
